title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: In recent years, a movement known as minimalism has gained significant traction, especially among younger generations. This lifestyle choice involves stripping (0) one's possessions to the bare essentials. (9) is often overlooked is that minimalism is not merely about aesthetics, but about reclaiming time and mental space. Many proponents find themselves (10) a loss to explain why they ever felt the need to accumulate so much in the first place. || (11) it not for the influence of social media, however, the trend might not have spread so rapidly. (12) only has it influenced interior design, but it has also prompted a rethink of consumer habits. (13) the fact that buying less can be difficult in a consumerist society, many people are now prioritizing experiences over objects. Such (14) the success of this movement that major brands are now marketing 'minimalist' products. (15) provided that this shift is genuine and not just another passing fad, it could lead to a more sustainable future for the planet. Ultimately, there is (16) doubt that less can indeed be more.

questions.explanation: Nominal relative pronoun acting as the subject of the clause.
Part of the fixed expression 'at a loss'.
Inversion in a conditional structure (Second Conditional).
Negative inversion using the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Preposition used to introduce a contrast, followed by a noun phrase.
Inversion for emphasis: 'Such is/was the...' followed by a result clause with 'that'.
Adverb used to emphasize a condition in 'Only provided that'.
Part of the fixed phrase 'there is no/little doubt that'.
